chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 1287 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on Misc. No.18027/2025 2. For orders on office objection No.22 3. For hearing of Misc. No.11412/2025 4. For hearing of main case 30.09.2025 Messrs. Ammar Athar Saeed and Usman Alam, advocates for petitioner Messrs. Abdullah Munshi and Shajee Siddiqui, advocates for respondent Mr. Naeem Akhtar Talpur, Additional Advocate General Sindh Mr. Muhammad Akbar Khan, Assistant Attorney General Learned counsel for the petitioner pressed the petition to the extent of prayer clause “c” only. Per learned counsel notice per Section 68 of the Cantonment Act, 1924 had been issued, as available at page 29. The reply and reminder is available at pages 31-37. Per learned counsel hearing notice was received and hearing was attended, however, the objections of the petitioner were never adjudicated. Learned counsel states that the facts and circumstances of the case are squarely covered by Division Bench judgment of this Court reported as 2022 CLC 2002. Learned counsel states that the impugned demand be set aside inter alia on account of being premature. He further states that the competent authority be pleased to adjudicate the objections raised by the petitioner while providing opportunity of hearing and thereafter pass an speaking order in such regard. He further seeks that pending the aforesaid no further coercive action be taken there against. Respective learned counsel for respondents articulates no cavil to the aforesaid and states that the petition be disposed of in the above terms.

He further seeks that pending the aforesaid no further coercive action be taken there against. Respective learned counsel for respondents articulates no cavil to the aforesaid and states that the petition be disposed of in the above terms. Order accordingly. Judge Judge Amjad
